Data Platform Strategic Update

The Data Platform is currently in an "unmasking" phase where architectural rigor is exposing years of technical debt previously hidden by manual spreadsheet patches (#8). While this clarity is essential, the volume of logic discrepancies has necessitated moving the All Active Report validation target to 2/17 (#1).

To navigate this, the leadership team is managing three primary pressure points:

- SME Dependency: Progress remains critically tied to a single "Point of Failure" legacy SME, which represents our most acute resource risk (#3).

- Architectural Capacity: The Lead Architect is currently "broad-fronted" across all integrations. We are attempting to mitigate this by pulling technical leads from HousingDB and FinAidSys to assist in the intensive legacy data cleanup (#4, #6).

- Exhaustive Reconciliation Shift: We have shifted our strategy to prioritize Exhaustive Legacy Reconciliation. The team is now focused on matching every legacy report discrepancy to ensure 100% parity with existing manual outputs. This deep-dive remediation has become the primary priority, deferring the "Workflow-Driven Discovery" and shifting our alignment milestones beyond 3/10 (#2, #5, #7).

Bottom Line: We are rejecting the 95% parity threshold in favor of a 100% Legacy Alignment goal. We are committing our current capacity to fixing historical logic errors within the legacy system, acknowledging that this focus on "past truth" will delay the foundational build-out for the 2027 NextGenSIS launch.
